title: pilosella × bifurca subsp. comata
intro: Pilosella × bifurca subsp. comata is a rare and hybrid plant species, part of the Rosaceae family, resulting from the cross between Pilosella bifurca and Pilosella officinarum. Traditionally, Pilosella × bifurca subsp. comata has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant's health benefits include aiding in wound healing, reducing fever, and treating various digestive issues, such as diarrhea and dysentery. Herbal preparations of Pilosella × bifurca subsp. comata can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use and availability may vary due to its rarity.
